disclaimer: Curated guide. Admission fees are approximate 2025 adult prices and do change (e.g. Himeji Castle revises fees March 2026); coordinates are approximate. Verify hours/fees before visiting.

tokyo.name: Tokyo

highlights.name: Senso-ji & Nakamise-dori
highlights.why: Tokyo's oldest temple; arrive before 9am to beat crowds

highlights.name: Meiji Jingu
highlights.why: Forest shrine next to Harajuku; serene cedar-lined approach

highlights.name: Shibuya Sky
highlights.why: Open-air rooftop over the Scramble; book sunset slot ahead

highlights.name: teamLab Planets (Toyosu)
highlights.why: Immersive barefoot digital art; timed tickets sell out

hidden_gems.name: Yanaka Ginza
hidden_gems.why: Old shitamachi shopping street that survived the war; cat statues and croquettes

hidden_gems.name: Kagurazaka
hidden_gems.why: Former geisha quarter with cobbled lanes and French-Japanese bistros

hidden_gems.name: Shimokitazawa
hidden_gems.why: Vintage shops, tiny bars and live houses; Tokyo's indie neighborhood

food_specialties: Depachika food halls for picnic supplies

seasonal.spring: Sakura peak usually late March-early April: Meguro River, Ueno Park, Chidorigafuchi rowboats.
seasonal.summer: Sumida River fireworks late July; very humid - plan indoor afternoons.
seasonal.autumn: Ginkgo gold at Meiji Jingu Gaien late November.
seasonal.winter: Crisp and clear; best Fuji visibility from observation decks.

tips: Get a Suica/Pasmo in Apple/Google Wallet on arrival
Tokyo Metro 24/48/72h passes pay off at 3+ rides/day

kyoto.name: Kyoto

highlights.name: Fushimi Inari Taisha
highlights.why: 10,000 torii gates; go at dawn or after 17:00, upper trails near-empty

highlights.name: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
highlights.why: Zen pavilion sheathed in gold leaf over a mirror pond

highlights.name: Kiyomizu-dera
highlights.why: Hillside wooden stage with city views; pair with Sannenzaka lanes

highlights.name: Arashiyama bamboo grove + Tenryu-ji
highlights.why: Bamboo corridor (free) beside a UNESCO zen garden (500)

hidden_gems.name: Honen-in
hidden_gems.why: Mossy thatched gate off the Philosopher's Path; raked sand mounds, almost no visitors

hidden_gems.name: Otagi Nenbutsu-ji
hidden_gems.why: 1,200 moss-covered rakan statues, each with a different face; far end of Arashiyama

hidden_gems.name: Fushimi sake district
hidden_gems.why: Canal-side breweries south of the city; Gekkeikan museum tasting ~600

seasonal.spring: Sakura peak ~April 1-10: Philosopher's Path, Maruyama Park; book everything early.
seasonal.summer: Gion Matsuri all July (grand processions 17th & 24th); kawadoko river dining.
seasonal.autumn: Foliage mid-November-early December: Tofuku-ji, Eikando night illumination.
seasonal.winter: Quiet season; occasional snow on Kinkaku-ji is spectacular.

tips: Buses get jammed - prefer subway + walking
One-day subway+bus pass 1,100 pays off quickly

osaka.name: Osaka

highlights.name: Dotonbori
highlights.why: Neon canyon of street food under the Glico sign; go after dark

highlights.name: Osaka Castle
highlights.why: Reconstructed keep with museum and skyline views from the top floor

highlights.name: Kuromon Ichiba Market
highlights.why: Eat-as-you-go market: wagyu skewers, uni, grilled scallops

highlights.name: Umeda Sky Building
highlights.why: Floating garden observatory linking twin towers

hidden_gems.name: Hozenji Yokocho
hidden_gems.why: Lantern-lit stone alley with the moss-covered Fudo statue - splash water on it for luck

hidden_gems.name: Nakazakicho
hidden_gems.why: Bombing-spared blocks of pre-war houses turned retro cafes and zakka shops

hidden_gems.name: Shinsekai & Janjan Yokocho
hidden_gems.why: Retro 1912 district under Tsutenkaku tower; standing kushikatsu bars

seasonal.spring: Sakura along Okawa river & Kema Sakuranomiya park, late March-early April.
seasonal.summer: Tenjin Matsuri July 24-25 with river procession and fireworks.
seasonal.autumn: Minoo Park waterfall hike (30 min by Hankyu) for maple leaves + momiji tempura.
seasonal.winter: Illuminations along Midosuji; kuidaore food crawls are indoor-friendly.

tips: 'Kuidaore' (eat till you drop) is the city motto - budget extra food money here
Osaka Metro day pass 820 on weekdays

nara.name: Nara

highlights.name: Todai-ji Daibutsuden
highlights.why: World's largest bronze Buddha in a vast wooden hall

highlights.name: Nara Park deer
highlights.why: 1,200 sacred bowing deer; crackers 200/pack

highlights.name: Kasuga Taisha
highlights.why: Vermilion shrine hung with 3,000 bronze and stone lanterns

hidden_gems.name: Naramachi
hidden_gems.why: Edo-period merchant quarter: machiya lattice houses, tiny museums, mochi shops

hidden_gems.name: Yoshikien Garden
hidden_gems.why: Three gardens in one - free admission for foreign visitors

hidden_gems.name: Wakakusa-yama
hidden_gems.why: Grassy hill behind the park; 30-min climb for sunset over the basin

seasonal.spring: Deer + sakura in Nara Park is a classic combo, early April.
seasonal.summer: Lantern festivals (Mantoro) at Kasuga Taisha in mid-August.
seasonal.autumn: Sub-temple gardens turn red through late November.
seasonal.winter: Yamayaki (mountain burning festival) on Wakakusa-yama, fourth Saturday of January.

tips: Easy half/full-day trip from Kyoto or Osaka; main sights walkable from Kintetsu-Nara

hiroshima.name: Hiroshima

highlights.name: Peace Memorial Park & Museum
highlights.why: Moving, essential history; museum entry just 200

highlights.name: Itsukushima Shrine (Miyajima)
highlights.why: The floating torii; check tide tables - high tide for floating, low to walk out

highlights.name: Hiroshima Castle
highlights.why: Reconstructed 'Carp Castle' with samurai-era museum

hidden_gems.name: Mitaki-dera
hidden_gems.why: Mossy hillside temple with three waterfalls, 15 min by train - locals only

hidden_gems.name: Shukkei-en
hidden_gems.why: 1620 'shrunken scenery' garden that quietly survived the bomb

hidden_gems.name: Okonomimura
hidden_gems.why: Three floors, 23 okonomiyaki counters - pick whichever grill has locals

seasonal.spring: Sakura around the castle moat and Peace Park, late March-early April.
seasonal.summer: Hot; Miyajima evenings are the escape - torii lit until 23:00.
seasonal.autumn: Momijidani valley on Miyajima is one of Japan's best maple spots, mid-late November.
seasonal.winter: Oyster season peak - try kakigoya grill shacks.

tips: Stay on Miyajima past 17:00 - day-trippers leave and deer roam the lantern-lit shore

kanazawa.name: Kanazawa

highlights.name: Kenroku-en
highlights.why: One of Japan's three great gardens; go at opening (7:00)

highlights.name: Higashi Chaya District
highlights.why: Geisha teahouse streets; gold-leaf everything

highlights.name: Omicho Market
highlights.why: 180 stalls; kaisendon breakfast is the move

hidden_gems.name: D.T. Suzuki Museum
hidden_gems.why: Minimalist water-mirror courtyard honoring the zen philosopher - meditative and empty

hidden_gems.name: Myoryu-ji (Ninja Temple)
hidden_gems.why: Trapdoors, hidden stairs, escape tunnels; reserve a tour by phone

hidden_gems.name: Oyama Shrine
hidden_gems.why: Shrine gate mixing Dutch stained glass with Japanese joinery

seasonal.spring: Sakura along the castle moats ~April 5-15, later than Tokyo.
seasonal.summer: Hyakumangoku Festival, first weekend of June.
seasonal.winter: Yukitsuri snow-ropes over Kenroku-en pines; snow-dusted chaya streets; crab season.

tips: Loop bus day pass 800 links all sights
Most museums closed Tuesdays

hakone.name: Hakone

highlights.name: Hakone Open-Air Museum
highlights.why: Sculpture park in the mountains: Picasso pavilion, foot bath

highlights.name: Owakudani
highlights.why: Steaming volcanic valley via ropeway; black eggs 500/5

highlights.name: Hakone Shrine & Lake Ashi
highlights.why: Lakeside torii photo spot; pirate-ship cruise across the caldera

hidden_gems.name: Amazake-chaya
hidden_gems.why: 400-year-old thatched teahouse on the old Tokaido; amazake + chikara-mochi ~900

hidden_gems.name: Old Tokaido stone path
hidden_gems.why: Edo-period cedar-lined highway segment between Moto-Hakone and the teahouse

hidden_gems.name: Gora Park
hidden_gems.why: French-style fountain garden; quiet craft workshops

seasonal.summer: Hydrangea train on the Tozan railway, June-July.
seasonal.autumn: Susuki pampas fields at Sengokuhara, gold in late October.
seasonal.winter: Clearest Fuji views from Owakudani and Lake Ashi.

tips: Hakone Freepass (5,000 from Odawara / 6,100 from Shinjuku, 2 days) covers ropeway, boat, buses - almost always worth it
Do the loop clockwise to beat tour groups

nikko.name: Nikko

highlights.name: Toshogu Shrine
highlights.why: Ieyasu's gilded mausoleum: sleeping cat, three monkeys, 5-story pagoda

highlights.name: Shinkyo Bridge
highlights.why: Sacred vermilion bridge at the mountain gateway

highlights.name: Kegon Falls
highlights.why: 97m plunge from Lake Chuzenji; elevator to the basin 570

hidden_gems.name: Kanmangafuchi Abyss
hidden_gems.why: Riverside row of ~70 jizo statues in red caps; said to be uncountable

hidden_gems.name: Tamozawa Imperial Villa
hidden_gems.why: 106-room imperial summer residence blending Edo and Meiji styles

seasonal.spring: Late sakura mid-April; shrine cedar avenues fresh green.
seasonal.summer: 10°C cooler than Tokyo - classic summer retreat.
seasonal.autumn: Irohazaka switchbacks blaze mid-October-early November (expect traffic).
seasonal.winter: Snow-quiet shrines; Chuzenji onsen ice formations.

tips: Tobu Nikko Pass from Asakusa bundles fare + local buses
World Heritage area is hilly - allow a full day

kamakura.name: Kamakura

highlights.name: Great Buddha (Kotoku-in)
highlights.why: 13.5m bronze Amida sitting open-air since 1495

highlights.name: Tsurugaoka Hachimangu
highlights.why: Samurai-era shrine anchoring the town axis

highlights.name: Hasedera
highlights.why: Eleven-headed Kannon, hydrangea path, bay views

hidden_gems.name: Hokokuji
hidden_gems.why: Small zen temple with a bamboo grove; matcha in the grove ~600

hidden_gems.name: Ten-en hiking trail
hidden_gems.why: Ridge walk linking Kencho-ji to Zuisen-ji - temples + woods in 90 min

hidden_gems.name: Gokurakuji
hidden_gems.why: Humble thatched-gate temple beloved by film-makers; almost no tourists

seasonal.spring: Dankazura approach lined with sakura, late March-early April.
seasonal.summer: Hydrangeas at Meigetsu-in & Hasedera in June (very crowded weekends).
seasonal.autumn: Late foliage - early December at Engaku-ji.
seasonal.winter: Mild; clear views of Fuji from Inamuragasaki beach.

tips: Ride the Enoden tram car to Hase/Enoshima - it hugs the sea wall
Arrive before 10am on weekends

takayama.name: Takayama

highlights.name: Sanmachi Suji
highlights.why: Edo merchant streets: dark-wood sake breweries with cedar-ball signs

highlights.name: Miyagawa Morning Market
highlights.why: Riverside farm stalls till noon; hida-beef buns and pickles

highlights.name: Hida Folk Village
highlights.why: 30 relocated gassho-zukuri thatched farmhouses on a hillside

hidden_gems.name: Higashiyama Walking Course
hidden_gems.why: Temple-to-temple path through the old town's eastern hills - usually deserted

hidden_gems.name: Sake brewery hopping
hidden_gems.why: Six breweries in Sanmachi pour 100-300 tasting cups in winter

seasonal.spring: Spring Takayama Matsuri April 14-15 - ornate festival floats.
seasonal.summer: Cool mountain base for Kamikochi hiking.
seasonal.autumn: Autumn Matsuri October 9-10; foliage late October.
seasonal.winter: Deep snow, sake season; day trip to Shirakawa-go illuminations (book buses early).

tips: Everything is walkable from the station
Pair with Shirakawa-go by Nohi bus (~50 min)

nagoya.name: Nagoya

highlights.name: Nagoya Castle & Honmaru Palace
highlights.why: Golden shachihoko dolphins; lavishly rebuilt palace interiors

highlights.name: Atsuta Jingu
highlights.why: Shrine of the sacred sword, one of Shinto's holiest sites

highlights.name: Osu Shopping District
highlights.why: 1,200 shops mixing temple market, electronics and vintage

hidden_gems.name: Tokugawa-en
hidden_gems.why: Daimyo stroll garden next to the shogunal art museum

hidden_gems.name: Endoji shotengai
hidden_gems.why: Faded Showa arcade with standout coffee and craft beer

food_specialties: Morning service: coffee buys free toast

seasonal.spring: Sakura along the castle moats early April.
seasonal.summer: Very hot; Sekai no Yamachan beer halls at night.

tips: Often done as a shinkansen stopover - station lockers + 4 hours covers the core

fukuoka.name: Fukuoka

highlights.name: Yatai stalls (Tenjin/Nakasu)
highlights.why: Open-air food stalls along the river - Fukuoka's signature night out

highlights.name: Ohori Park
highlights.why: Lake loop + castle ruins + superb modern art museum next door

highlights.name: Canal City Hakata
highlights.why: Canal-threaded mall with ramen stadium on 5F

hidden_gems.name: Nanzoin
hidden_gems.why: 41m bronze reclining Buddha in forested Sasaguri, 25 min by local train

hidden_gems.name: Yanagibashi Rengo Market
hidden_gems.why: 'Fukuoka's kitchen' - tiny fish market where chefs shop, zero tourists

seasonal.spring: Sakura at Maizuru Park (castle ruins) late March.
seasonal.summer: Hakata Gion Yamakasa race, July 1-15, 4:59am finale.
seasonal.autumn: Mild; oyster huts open along Itoshima coast.
seasonal.winter: Mentaiko and motsunabe season; mild for Japan.

tips: Yatai etiquette: order one drink + one dish minimum, don't linger
Airport is 5 min by subway - easiest big-city airport in Japan

sapporo.name: Sapporo

highlights.name: Odori Park & TV Tower
highlights.why: The city's green spine; festival ground year-round

highlights.name: Sapporo Beer Museum
highlights.why: Brick brewery heritage; tasting set ~800

highlights.name: Mt. Moiwa ropeway
highlights.why: One of Japan's official top-3 night views

hidden_gems.name: Moerenuma Park
hidden_gems.why: Isamu Noguchi's land-art park - glass pyramid, sculpted hills

hidden_gems.name: Nijo Market
hidden_gems.why: Compact morning market; uni-ikura don breakfast

seasonal.spring: Late sakura - early May at Maruyama Park (BBQ under blossoms is local custom).
seasonal.summer: Best season: 25°C, lavender day trips to Furano in July.
seasonal.autumn: Autumn Fest food stalls in Odori, September.
seasonal.winter: Yuki Matsuri snow festival early February; powder season.

tips: Streets are a numbered grid - navigation is trivial
Underground walkways link everything in winter

sendai.name: Sendai

highlights.name: Zuihoden Mausoleum
highlights.why: Date Masamune's ornate cedar-forest tomb

highlights.name: Aoba Castle site
highlights.why: Masamune's equestrian statue over the city panorama

highlights.name: Jozenji-dori
highlights.why: Zelkova-lined avenue, the 'City of Trees' postcard

hidden_gems.name: Gyutan alley (station 3F)
hidden_gems.why: Row of beef-tongue specialists inside the station - locals queue at Rikyu

hidden_gems.name: Akiu Great Falls
hidden_gems.why: 55m waterfall + onsen town, 50 min by bus

seasonal.summer: Tanabata Festival August 6-8 - giant paper streamers citywide.
seasonal.autumn: Day trip to Naruko Gorge, peak late October.
seasonal.winter: Pageant of Starlight illumination on Jozenji-dori, December.

tips: Loople sightseeing bus day pass 630 hits all the Date sites

matsumoto.name: Matsumoto

highlights.name: Matsumoto Castle
highlights.why: Japan's oldest surviving keep - the black 'Crow Castle' against the Alps

highlights.name: Nakamachi Street
highlights.why: Black-and-white kura storehouses turned craft shops

hidden_gems.name: Nawate-dori
hidden_gems.why: Riverside 'frog street' of tiny stalls - taiyaki and antiques

hidden_gems.name: Matsumoto City Museum of Art
hidden_gems.why: Hometown museum of Yayoi Kusama - polka-dot everything

seasonal.spring: Castle moat sakura + Alps snowcaps, mid-April - the photo.
seasonal.winter: Ice sculpture festival at the castle, January.

tips: Castle queue builds by 10am in season - go at opening

himeji.name: Himeji

highlights.name: Himeji Castle
highlights.why: Japan's finest original castle, the White Heron; fee rises for non-residents from March 2026

highlights.name: Koko-en
highlights.why: Nine Edo-style gardens beside the castle; combo ticket 1,050

hidden_gems.name: Mt. Shosha Engyo-ji
hidden_gems.why: Mountaintop temple complex from The Last Samurai; ropeway + forest paths

seasonal.spring: 1,000 sakura around the castle - one of Japan's top blossom scenes, early April.
seasonal.summer: Castle is bright but hot; go early.

tips: Doable as a 3-4h shinkansen stopover between Osaka and Hiroshima - castle is 15 min walk straight up the boulevard
